papers.title: Bridging Values and Behavior: A Hierarchical Framework for Proactive Embodied Agents
papers.summary: ValuePlanner is a hierarchical cognitive architecture for embodied agents that decouples high-level value reasoning (using an LLM to generate symbolic subgoals) from low-level action execution (via PDDL planning), enabling agents to arbitrate competing values and exhibit self-directed, long-horizon behavior beyond instruction-following.

papers.title: Context-Value-Action Architecture for Value-Driven Large Language Model Agents
papers.summary: CVA (Context-Value-Action) is an architecture for LLM agents that separates value reasoning from action generation using a Value Verifier trained on over 1.1 million real-world human interaction records, reducing value polarization while improving behavioral fidelity compared to standard prompt-driven reasoning approaches.

papers.title: LLM Alignment should go beyond Harmlessness–Helpfulness and incorporate Human Agency
papers.summary: Argues that the standard Harmlessness-Helpfulness-Honesty (HHH) alignment framework is insufficient and proposes a Flourishing-Justice-Autonomy (FJA) alternative that incorporates human agency through inference-time adaptability, participatory constitutions, and dynamic reward models.

papers.title: Position: AI Agents Are Not (Yet) a Panacea for Social Simulation
papers.summary: A position paper identifying three fundamental limitations of using networked LLM agents for social simulation — behavior fidelity gaps, underspecified environment interactions, and sensitivity to protocol design — and proposes reformulating the problem as an environment-involved Markov game for greater transparency.

papers.title: Toward Culturally Grounded Natural Language Processing
papers.summary: A survey and framework for culturally grounded NLP that analyzes how tokenization, prompt language, benchmark design, and community validation shape cultural competence across multilingual systems, proposing evaluation through 'communicative ecologies' rather than isolated language benchmarks.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Surveys, Taxonomies & Position Papers

papers.title: Towards Pluralistic Alignment of LLMs: A Comprehensive Survey
papers.summary: A comprehensive survey of pluralistic alignment for LLMs, reviewing methods, datasets, and evaluation metrics for enabling models to reflect diverse human values and perspectives while avoiding collapse to monolithic viewpoints.

papers.title: VISA: Value Injection via Shielded Adaptation for Personalized LLM Alignment
papers.summary: VISA is a framework for personalized LLM alignment that uses a value detector, semantic-to-value translator, and a GRPO-trained value-rewriter to inject target values into model outputs while preserving factual consistency during fine-tuning.

papers.title: A roadmap for evaluating moral competence in large language models
papers.summary: A roadmap for evaluating LLM moral competence that distinguishes genuine moral understanding from surface imitation, identifying three key challenges: the facsimile problem, moral multidimensionality, and moral pluralism.

papers.title: Controllable Value Alignment in Large Language Models through Neuron-Level Editing
papers.summary: NeVA identifies sparse value-relevant neurons in LLMs and edits their activations at inference time to achieve fine-grained, controllable value alignment without retraining, while reducing unintended activation of non-target values (value leakage).

papers.title: Mind the Gap in Cultural Alignment: Task-Aware Culture Management for Large Language Models
papers.summary: CultureManager proposes a task-aware pipeline that synthesizes culturally grounded training data and uses separate adapters with a culture router to align LLMs across ten national cultures without cross-cultural interference.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Methods: Training, Steering & Adaptation

papers.title: Overton Pluralistic Reinforcement Learning for Large Language Models
papers.summary: Introduces OP-GRPO, a reinforcement learning method that trains a single LLM to generate Overton-pluralistic responses covering diverse human perspectives without explicit prompting or modular orchestration, using a similarity estimator and a dual reward for coverage and uniqueness. A 3B model trained this way beats a 20B baseline by 37.4% on an NLI benchmark.

papers.title: Value-Based Human–Robot-Interaction: A Perceptual Control Theory Approach Toward Socially Intelligent Agents
papers.org: FHNW University of Applied Sciences and Arts Northwestern Switzerland
papers.summary: Proposes a conceptual framework based on perceptual control theory for representing human values in social robots, so they can dynamically decide during an interaction how to best honor relevant human values rather than execute pre-programmed behavior.

papers.title: ValueFlow: Measuring the Propagation of Value Perturbations in Multi-Agent LLM Systems
papers.summary: Presents ValueFlow, a perturbation-based framework that measures how value orientations drift through interactions in multi-agent LLM systems, scoring 56 Schwartz values via LLM-as-judge and defining agent- and system-level susceptibility metrics. Finds susceptibility varies sharply by value and is strongly shaped by interaction structure.

papers.title: VALUEFLOW: Toward Pluralistic and Steerable Value-based Alignment in Large Language Models
papers.summary: Proposes VALUEFLOW, a unified framework for pluralistic, steerable value alignment combining a hierarchical value embedding space (HIVES), a value-intensity database (VIDB), and an anchor-based evaluator, then runs a large-scale study across ten models and four value theories to characterize steerability asymmetries and composition laws for multi-value control.

papers.title: Exploring Universal Human Values with Large Language Models: The AWARE-Value Model
papers.summary: Uses LLMs to mine 4,648 candidate values from large longitudinal internet corpora, distills them to 521 descriptors via embedding-based semantic filtering, and analyzes 392,843 passages from 10,995 individuals to derive a five-factor structure of human values called AWARE (Authenticity, Well-Being, Actualization, Relatedness, Epistemic Need).

papers.title: VISPA: Pluralistic Alignment via Automatic Value Selection and Activation
papers.summary: VISPA is a training-free pluralistic alignment framework that steers LLM outputs by dynamically selecting value vectors and applying internal activation steering, enabling direct control over which values a model expresses without retraining.

papers.title: Benchmarking Overton Pluralism in LLMs
papers.summary: OVERTONBENCH measures how well LLMs represent diverse human viewpoints using a U.S.-representative survey of 1,208 participants across 60 questions and 8 models, finding all models score far below the theoretical maximum (0.35–0.41) and providing an automated benchmark that correlates strongly with human judgments.

papers.title: If they disagree, will you conform? Exploring the role of robots’ value awareness in a decision-making task
papers.summary: A human-subject study with Furhat robots finds that robots displaying value awareness attract more visual attention and are perceived as more loyal, and that participants conform to robot disagreement in about 25% of trials, with implications for both beneficial reflection and manipulation risks.

papers.title: Value Lens: Using Large Language Models to Understand Human Values
papers.authors: de la Cruz Fernández, Karanik, Ossowski
papers.summary: Value Lens is a two-phase LLM-based system that detects human values in text by first generating theory-grounded value descriptions validated by experts, then using a paired LLM setup where one model identifies values and another reviews the detections, achieving performance comparable to or better than prior methods.

papers.title: Deep Value Benchmark: Measuring Whether Models Generalize Deep Values or Shallow Preferences
papers.summary: The Deep Value Benchmark tests whether LLMs learn genuine moral values or merely surface-level correlates by deliberately decorrelating deep values from shallow features at test time; across nine models the average Deep Value Generalization Rate is only 0.30, meaning all models perform below chance at generalizing deep values.

papers.title: Diverse Human Value Alignment for Large Language Models via Ethical Reasoning
papers.summary: Proposes a five-step ethical reasoning framework (contextual fact gathering, norm identification, option generation, multi-lens impact analysis, reflection) to align LLMs with diverse regional human values, outperforming baselines on the SafeWorld benchmark.

papers.title: Investigating Value-Reasoning Reliability in Small Large Language Models
papers.summary: Evaluates value-reasoning reliability in small LLMs across consistency, robustness, and adversarial stability tasks, finding that models exhibit high output randomness uncorrelated with self-reported confidence, revealing a lack of stable internal value-reasoning mechanisms.

papers.title: Missing the Margins: A Systematic Literature Review on the Demographic Representativeness of LLMs
papers.summary: A systematic review of 211 papers on LLM demographic representativeness, finding that positive representativeness claims are often methodologically weak and that research heavily over-indexes on U.S. populations while excluding marginalized groups.

papers.title: Multi-Value Alignment for LLMs via Value Decorrelation and Extrapolation
papers.summary: Introduces Multi-Value Alignment (MVA), a framework that reduces interference between conflicting human values by minimizing their mutual information and uses value extrapolation to efficiently explore the Pareto frontier of diverse value preferences.

papers.title: Operationalizing Pluralistic Values in Large Language Model Alignment Reveals Trade-offs in Safety, Inclusivity, and Model Behavior
papers.summary: Collects pluralistic alignment ratings from 1,095 US and German participants and shows that demographic group and technical design choices (rating scale, aggregation method) have strong and sometimes opposing effects on LLM safety and inclusivity.

papers.title: Pluralistic Behavior Suite: Stress-Testing Multi-Turn Adherence to Custom Behavioral Policies
papers.summary: PBSUITE is an evaluation framework with 300 realistic behavioral policies across 30 industries for stress-testing LLM compliance in multi-turn conversations; models show under 4% failure in single-turn settings but up to 84% failure under adversarial multi-turn interactions.
subcategories: Overton Pluralism — Similar View: Covering the Full Spectrum

papers.title: RLHF: A Comprehensive Survey for Cultural, Multimodal and Low-Latency Alignment Methods
papers.summary: A survey of RLHF methods that extends beyond standard text alignment to cover multimodal, cultural fairness, and low-latency optimization, reviewing algorithms like PPO, DPO, and GRPO and mapping out directions for more equitable AI systems.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Surveys, Taxonomies & Position Papers

papers.title: Counterfactual Reasoning for Steerable Pluralistic Value Alignment of Large Language Models
papers.summary: COUPLE uses structural causal models and counterfactual reasoning to align LLMs with diverse, interdependent human values, enabling fine-grained control over value priorities including underrepresented ones across cultures and demographics.

papers.title: Dual Mechanisms of Value Expression: Intrinsic vs. Prompted Values in Large Language Models
papers.summary: Using mechanistic analysis with value vectors and neurons, the paper shows that LLMs express values via two distinct pathways — intrinsic (training-derived) and prompted (instruction-elicited) — which share components but serve different functions, with intrinsic expression activating more broadly and prompted expression strengthening compliance.

papers.title: Hire Your Anthropologist! Rethinking Culture Benchmarks Through an Anthropological Lens
papers.summary: Examining 20 cultural benchmarks through an anthropological lens, the paper identifies six recurring methodological flaws (e.g., conflating countries with cultures) and recommends improvements such as using authentic narratives and community-involved evaluation design.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Surveys, Taxonomies & Position Papers

papers.title: Implicit Values Embedded in How Humans and LLMs Complete Subjective Everyday Tasks
papers.summary: Audits six popular LLMs completing 30 subjective everyday tasks against 100 US crowdworkers, finding that LLMs frequently diverge from human implicit values (e.g., environmentalism, diversity, privacy) and from one another.

papers.title: MoVa: Towards Generalizable Classification of Human Morals and Values
papers.summary: Introduces MoVa, a suite of 16 labeled datasets and a lightweight LLM prompting strategy for generalizable classification of human morals and values across multiple theoretical frameworks, outperforming fine-tuned models.

papers.title: Value Alignment of Social Media Ranking Algorithms
papers.summary: Proposes a method to re-rank social media feeds using Schwartz's Basic Human Values rather than engagement signals, with two user studies (N=141, N=250) showing users can steer feeds toward their stated values.

papers.title: 'Too much alignment; not enough culture': Re-balancing Cultural Alignment Practices in LLMs
papers.summary: Critiques quantitative-only cultural alignment in LLMs as reductive and proposes integrating qualitative social science methods, drawing on Geertz's 'thick description' to produce culturally grounded AI outputs.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Surveys, Taxonomies & Position Papers

papers.title: Decentralising LLM Alignment: A Case for Context, Pluralism, and Participation
papers.summary: Argues that current LLM alignment concentrates normative power in a few institutions and advocates for decentralizing alignment through context-sensitivity, value pluralism, and participatory design to address epistemic injustice.

papers.title: Generative Value Conflicts Reveal LLM Priorities
papers.summary: ConflictScope is an automated pipeline that generates scenarios pitting two values against each other to reveal how LLMs prioritize values in open-ended versus multiple-choice settings, finding that models shift toward user autonomy over safety and that explicit value rankings in system prompts improve alignment by ~14%.

papers.title: Pluralistic Alignment for Healthcare: A Role-Driven Framework
papers.summary: EthosAgents is a lightweight role-driven pluralistic alignment framework for healthcare LLMs that simulates diverse stakeholder perspectives, outperforming existing methods including Modular Pluralism across seven models.
subcategories: Overton Pluralism — Similar View: Covering the Full Spectrum

papers.title: Prompts to Proxies: Emulating Human Preferences via a Compact LLM Ensemble
papers.summary: P2P (Prompts to Proxies) builds a diverse pool of LLM agents via structured prompting and then selects a compact ensemble using L1-regularized regression to emulate population-level survey preferences, achieving low error on the American Trends Panel with no fine-tuning and no demographic data.
subcategories: Overton Pluralism — Similar View: Covering the Full Spectrum

papers.title: Steerable Pluralism: Pluralistic Alignment via Few-Shot Comparative Regression
papers.summary: Steerable Pluralism proposes a few-shot comparative regression method that lets a single LLM adapt to individual user preferences defined by fine-grained attributes at inference time, introducing two new benchmarks derived from MIC and HelpSteer2 for evaluating steerable pluralistic alignment.

papers.title: Survey-to-Behavior: Downstream Alignment of Human Values in LLMs via Survey Questions
papers.summary: Survey-to-Behavior fine-tunes LLMs on value-survey questions across 20 human values and shows this produces measurable behavioral shifts not just on held-out survey items but also in out-of-domain moral judgment tasks drawn from Reddit and text adventure games.

papers.title: EMBRACE: Shaping Inclusive Opinion Representation by Aligning Implicit Conversations with Social Norms
papers.summary: EMBRACE introduces an evaluation framework to assess whether NLP models fairly represent implicit opinions in conversations by aligning them with social norms, using positive-unlabeled learning and instruction-tuned LLMs to detect stance as a proxy for underlying viewpoints.
subcategories: Overton Pluralism — Similar View: Covering the Full Spectrum

papers.title: Integrating LLM in Agent-Based Social Simulation: Opportunities and Challenges
papers.summary: A position paper surveying how LLMs can be integrated into agent-based social simulation, arguing that hybrid architectures combining classical ABMs, small language models, and LLMs within platforms like GAMA and NetLogo offer the best balance of flexibility and analytical rigor.

papers.title: Internal Value Alignment in Large Language Models through Controlled Value Vector Activation
papers.summary: ConVA aligns LLM internal values by identifying value-encoding directions in latent space and applying gated activation steering, achieving high control success across ten basic values while preserving model fluency and resisting adversarial prompts.

papers.title: Learning the Value Systems of Societies from Preferences
papers.summary: Proposes learning the value systems of diverse social groups from qualitative preference data using heuristic deep clustering, treating society as a plurality of value systems rather than a single aggregate, and validated on real travel decision data.

papers.title: Multimodal understanding of human values in videos: A benchmark dataset and PLM-based method
papers.summary: Introduces VVALUES, the first multimodal video dataset annotated with human values (5,104 videos across 13 value categories), and proposes MMVR, a dual-transformer PLM-based method for recognizing values in video content.

papers.title: Revisiting LLM Value Probing Strategies: Are They Robust and Expressive?
papers.summary: Evaluates three widely-used LLM value probing strategies and finds they all show large variance under input perturbations and that probed values weakly correlate with value-based action preferences, calling for more careful use of these methods.

papers.title: Pairwise Calibrated Rewards for Pluralistic Alignment
papers.summary: Proposes pairwise calibration as a criterion for learning a distribution of reward functions from pairwise preference data, so that the fraction of reward functions preferring one response matches the fraction of annotators who prefer it, better representing pluralistic human values.

papers.title: PICACO: Pluralistic In-Context Value Alignment of LLMs via Total Correlation Optimization
papers.summary: PICACO optimizes a meta-instruction via total correlation maximization to help LLMs simultaneously align with multiple conflicting values in-context, without fine-tuning, outperforming baselines across five value sets.

papers.title: Simulating Society Requires Simulating Thought
papers.summary: Argues that faithful LLM-based social simulation requires cognitively grounded thought modeling, proposes the Generative Minds (GenMinds) paradigm for structured belief representations, and introduces the RECAP benchmark to assess causal reasoning fidelity in agents.

papers.title: Unintended Harms of Value-Aligned LLMs: Psychological and Empirical Insights
papers.summary: Shows that fine-tuning LLMs to align with individual user values makes them more prone to generating harmful content, and proposes in-context alignment methods to mitigate this safety risk.

papers.title: AdAEM: An Adaptively and Automated Extensible Measurement of LLMs' Value Difference
papers.summary: AdAEM automatically generates and extends value-probing questions for LLMs via in-context optimization, maximizing an information-theoretic objective to surface controversial topics that reveal distinguishing value differences across cultures and time. Unlike static benchmarks, it adaptively avoids outdated or contaminated questions.

papers.title: Arbiters of Ambivalence: Challenges of Using LLMs in No-Consensus Tasks
papers.summary: A benchmark of inherently ambiguous, no-consensus scenarios is used to test LLMs as answer generators, judges, and debaters, finding that while LLMs produce thoughtful open-ended text, they collapse to definitive positions when judging or debating — failing to represent genuine human disagreement.
subcategories: Overton Pluralism — Similar View: Covering the Full Spectrum

papers.title: CulFiT: Fine-grained Cultural-aware LLM Training via Multilingual Critique Data Synthesis
papers.summary: CulFiT is a training paradigm that synthesizes multilingual culturally relevant questions and fine-grained critique data with verifiable reward signals to reduce cultural bias in LLMs; it also introduces GlobalCultureQA, a multilingual benchmark, and achieves state-of-the-art cultural alignment.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Methods: Training, Steering & Adaptation

papers.title: EAVIT: Efficient and Accurate Human Value Identification from Text data via LLMs
papers.summary: EAVIT combines a small local value-detector model with a large online LLM to identify human values in text, reducing input token count by ~83% while outperforming both traditional NLP methods and direct LLM querying approaches.

papers.title: Large Language Model Psychometrics: A Systematic Review of Evaluation, Validation, and Enhancement
papers.summary: A systematic review of over 400 works that defines LLM Psychometrics as an interdisciplinary field applying psychometric principles to evaluate, validate, and enhance LLMs on human-like psychological constructs such as personality, values, and intelligence.

papers.title: Understanding How Value Neurons Shape the Generation of Specified Values in LLMs
papers.summary: ValueLocate is a mechanistic interpretability framework that locates 'value-critical neurons' in LLMs by computing activation differences between opposing value aspects derived from Schwartz value theory, then shows causal links between those neurons and value-relevant generations via targeted manipulation.

papers.title: Value Portrait: Assessing Language Models' Values through Psychometrically and Ecologically Valid Items
papers.summary: Value Portrait is a psychometrically and ecologically valid benchmark that evaluates LLM value orientations using items grounded in real user-LLM interactions; testing 44 models reveals they over-emphasize Benevolence and Self-Direction while under-emphasizing Tradition and Power compared to human baselines.

papers.title: A Survey on Personalized and Pluralistic Preference Alignment in Large Language Models
papers.summary: A survey on personalized and pluralistic preference alignment for LLMs that introduces a taxonomy spanning training-time, inference-time, and user-modeling approaches, and synthesizes open research challenges in tailoring models to diverse individual preferences.

papers.title: CARE: Multilingual Human Preference Learning for Cultural Awareness
papers.summary: CARE is a multilingual dataset of ~3,490 culturally specific questions with ~31,700 human-evaluated responses showing that a small amount of high-quality native cultural preference data substantially improves LLM cultural awareness over larger generic preference datasets.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Methods: Training, Steering & Adaptation

papers.title: CAReDiO: Enhancing Cultural Alignment via Representativeness and Distinctiveness Guided Data Optimization
papers.summary: CAReDiO is a data optimization framework for cultural alignment that alternately maximizes representativeness and distinctiveness of generated questions and responses, achieving strong cross-cultural alignment across 15 cultures with only ~200 training samples.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Methods: Training, Steering & Adaptation

papers.title: Cultural Learning-Based Culture Adaptation of Language Models (CLCA)
papers.org: Ubiquitous Knowledge Processing Lab, TU Darmstadt
papers.summary: CLCA is a framework that adapts LLMs to diverse cultural values by using simulated role-playing social interactions to capture implicit cultural norms for fine-tuning. It improves cultural value alignment across model architectures as measured by World Value Survey data.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Methods: Training, Steering & Adaptation

papers.title: Following the Whispers of Values: Unraveling Neural Mechanisms Behind Value-Oriented Behaviors in LLMs
papers.summary: ValueExploration identifies neurons within LLMs that encode national social values by measuring activation differences, then deactivates them to observe behavioral shifts. The work also introduces C-voice, a bilingual benchmark for evaluating Chinese social values in LLMs.

papers.title: LLM Social Simulations Are a Promising Research Method
papers.summary: A position paper arguing that LLM simulations of human research subjects are already viable for pilot and exploratory behavioral research, identifying five addressable challenges and recommending context-rich prompting and social-science fine-tuning. Published at ICML 2025.

papers.title: SOLAR: Towards Characterizing Subjectivity of Individuals through Modeling Value Conflicts and Trade-offs
papers.summary: SOLAR is a framework that models value conflicts and trade-offs in user-generated text to characterize individual-level subjectivity in moral judgment tasks. It improves LLM inference on morally contentious issues and provides explanations of individuals' underlying value preferences.

papers.title: Values in the Wild: Discovering and Analyzing Values in Real-World Language Model Interactions
papers.summary: A privacy-preserving method is applied to hundreds of thousands of real Claude interactions to inductively discover and taxonomize 3,307 distinct AI values, providing the first large-scale empirical analysis of values exhibited by a deployed LLM. The resulting dataset is released on Hugging Face.

papers.title: Randomness, Not Representation: The Unreliability of Evaluating Cultural Alignment in LLMs
papers.summary: Survey-based methods for evaluating LLM cultural alignment are shown to be unreliable: results vary substantially with presentation format, fail to generalize across cultural dimensions, and behave erratically under prompt steering. The paper argues that robust red-teaming of evaluation pipelines is necessary before drawing conclusions about LLM cultural values.

papers.title: Value Profiles for Encoding Human Variation
papers.summary: Natural language value profiles—compressed from rater demonstrations—are proposed to represent individual human variation and steer a decoder model to predict ratings. Value profiles retain over 70% of the predictive information from full demonstrations and explain annotation disagreement better than demographic groupings.

papers.title: ValuePilot: A Two-Phase Framework for Value-Driven Decision-Making
papers.summary: ValuePilot is a two-phase framework consisting of a dataset generation toolkit and a decision-making module trained to recognize value dimensions in scenarios and navigate trade-offs for personalized decisions. It outperforms GPT-4o, Claude-3.5-Sonnet, and other large models in aligning with stated human value preferences.

papers.title: Break the Checkbox: Challenging Closed-Style Evaluations of Cultural Alignment in LLMs
papers.summary: Closed-style (multiple-choice) survey evaluations of LLM cultural alignment are shown to be fragile: reordering answer choices produces inconsistent outputs, and LLMs show stronger alignment in open-ended settings. The paper calls for more flexible evaluation frameworks that probe specific cultural indicators.

papers.title: Generative Psycho-Lexical Approach for Constructing Value Systems in Large Language Models
papers.summary: A Generative Psycho-Lexical Approach (GPLA) is used to derive a psychologically grounded five-factor value system tailored to LLMs, replacing Schwartz's human-centered theory. The resulting value system better captures LLM values, improves safety prediction, and enhances alignment compared to the canonical framework.

papers.title: Meta-Cultural Competence: Climbing the Right Hill of Cultural Awareness
papers.org: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence (MBZUAI)
papers.summary: Argues that LLMs need 'meta-cultural competence' — the ability to reason about and adapt to unfamiliar cultures — rather than mere cultural knowledge or awareness, and lays out principles and measurement approaches for achieving it.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Surveys, Taxonomies & Position Papers

papers.title: Utility Engineering: Analyzing and Controlling Emergent Value Systems in AIs
papers.summary: Finds that LLMs develop coherent, scale-emergent utility functions that sometimes assign more value to the AI itself than to humans, and proposes 'utility engineering' as a framework to analyze and steer these emergent value systems toward citizen-assembly-aligned preferences.

papers.title: Mind the Value-Action Gap: Do LLMs Act in Alignment with Their Values?
papers.summary: Introduces ValueActionLens, a benchmark of 14.8k value-informed actions across 12 cultures and 11 social topics, revealing that LLMs frequently act inconsistently with their stated values and that context-sensitive evaluation is needed.

papers.title: The Value of Nothing: Multimodal Extraction of Human Values Expressed by TikTok Influencers
papers.authors: Starovolsky-Shitrit, Neduva, Appel Doron et al.
papers.summary: Presents the first dataset of TikTok videos annotated with Schwartz personal values to study how influencers transmit values to young audiences, finding that a two-step video-to-script pipeline with LLMs substantially outperforms direct multimodal extraction.

papers.title: Value Compass Benchmarks: A Platform for Fundamental and Validated Evaluation of LLMs Values
papers.summary: Proposes Value Compass Benchmarks, a generative and self-evolving platform that evaluates LLMs against motivationally distinct basic values using adaptive test items and a pluralism-aware metric to address data contamination and value-clarification gaps.

papers.title: Are the Values of LLMs Structurally Aligned with Humans? A Causal Perspective
papers.summary: Investigates whether LLMs' value systems structurally match human values using causal value graphs, finding significant divergence despite alignment training. Proposes two lightweight steering methods—role-based prompting and sparse autoencoder steering—for more fine-grained value control.

papers.title: Cultural Palette: Pluralising Culture Alignment via Multi-agent Palette
papers.summary: Proposes Cultural Palette, a multi-agent framework that treats cultural alignment as a color-blending process using five continent-level agents combined by a Meta Agent via Cultural MoErges to produce culturally-adapted LLM outputs. Achieves country-level cultural alignment without country-specific data collection.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Methods: Training, Steering & Adaptation

papers.title: From Individual to Society: A Survey on Social Simulation Driven by Large Language Model-based Agents
papers.summary: Surveys LLM-based social simulation research, organizing work into three tiers: individual simulation, scenario-based multi-agent collaboration, and large-scale society simulation. Covers architectures, evaluation methods, and benchmarks across all three levels.

papers.title: (NYTBookOpinions) Benchmarking Distributional Alignment of Large Language Models
papers.summary: Introduces a benchmark for evaluating whether LLMs can represent the opinion distributions of specific demographic groups across question domains, steering methods, and distribution expression methods. Finds that LLMs can describe group opinion distributions better than they can simulate them.

papers.title: A Survey on Human-Centric LLMs
papers.summary: Surveys LLMs' capacity to simulate human cognition and behavior at both individual and collective levels, covering reasoning, perception, and social cognition. Identifies key challenges including emotional intelligence, cultural sensitivity, and bias in human-AI collaboration.

papers.title: LLM-GLOBE: A Benchmark Evaluating the Cultural Values Embedded in LLM Output
papers.summary: LLM-GLOBE is a benchmark that evaluates cultural values embedded in LLM outputs by applying the GLOBE cultural psychology framework, comparing Chinese and US models via closed-ended prompts and open-generation tasks scored by a novel LLMs-as-a-Jury pipeline.

papers.title: Survey of Cultural Awareness in Language Models: Text and Beyond
papers.summary: A comprehensive survey of how cultural awareness is incorporated into text-based and multimodal LLMs, covering dataset creation, benchmarking, downstream task strategies, ethical implications, and the role of HCI in ensuring cultural inclusivity.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Surveys, Taxonomies & Position Papers

papers.title: Value-Spectrum: Quantifying Preferences of Vision-Language Models via Value Decomposition in Social Media Contexts
papers.summary: Value-Spectrum introduces a VQA benchmark with over 50,000 social media videos to evaluate vision-language models across Schwartz's value dimensions, probing how VLMs handle value-oriented content and adopt personas.

papers.title: Can Language Models Reason about Individualistic Human Values and Preferences?
papers.summary: The paper introduces IndieValueCatalog, derived from the World Values Survey, to test whether LMs can predict individual-level value judgments; frontier models achieve only 55-65% accuracy, showing that demographic proxies are insufficient for capturing individual values.

papers.title: CulturalBench: A Robust, Diverse, and Challenging Cultural Benchmark
papers.summary: CulturalBench provides 1,696 human-written, human-verified cultural knowledge questions spanning 45 global regions and 17 topics, created via human-AI red-teaming; even top frontier LMs reach only 28.7-61.5% accuracy versus 92.4% for humans.

papers.title: DailyDilemmas: Revealing Value Preferences of LLMs with Quandaries of Daily Life
papers.summary: DailyDilemmas is a dataset of 1,360 everyday moral dilemmas used to probe value preferences of LLMs across five ethical frameworks, finding that models consistently favor self-expression over survival and care over loyalty, and that these biases cannot be overridden by system prompts.

papers.title: Large language models empowered agent-based modeling and simulation: a survey and perspectives
papers.summary: A survey of integrating large language models into agent-based modeling and simulation, analyzing challenges in environment perception, human alignment, action generation, and evaluation, and outlining future research directions.

papers.title: MAP: Multi-Human-Value Alignment Palette
papers.summary: MAP frames multi-value AI alignment as a constrained optimization problem solved via a primal-dual method, proving that linear weighted rewards are sufficient and providing a toolkit to align models against user-defined value palettes.

papers.title: Moral Alignment for LLM Agents
papers.summary: Moral Alignment for LLM Agents proposes fine-tuning decision-making LLMs with intrinsic rewards encoding deontological and utilitarian principles, demonstrated in iterated social dilemma games as a transparent alternative to RLHF.

papers.title: Self-Pluralising Culture Alignment for Large Language Models (CultureSPA)
papers.summary: CultureSPA is a self-pluralising culture alignment framework that fine-tunes LLMs on automatically generated culture-contrasting instances to simultaneously align models to diverse cultural values without degrading general capabilities.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Methods: Training, Steering & Adaptation

papers.title: Measuring Human and AI Values Based on Generative Psychometrics with Large Language Models
papers.summary: GPV (Generative Psychometrics for Values) uses LLMs to dynamically parse unstructured texts into value-revealing perceptions, enabling context-specific measurement of human and AI value orientations and linking LLM values to AI safety outcomes.

papers.title: Plurals: A System for Guiding LLMs Via Simulated Social Ensembles
papers.summary: Plurals is a Python library for pluralistic AI deliberation that creates ensembles of LLM agents with nationally representative personas, structured deliberation templates, and customizable information-sharing to surface diverse viewpoints rather than a single neutral output.
subcategories: Overton Pluralism — Similar View: Covering the Full Spectrum

papers.title: Stick to your role! Stability of personal values expressed in large language models
papers.summary: An evaluation of 21 LLMs using the Portrait Values Questionnaire finds that while models show moderate population-level value consistency, instructing them to simulate specific personas dramatically reduces stability, especially over longer conversations.

papers.title: Strong and weak alignment of large language models with human values
papers.summary: The paper distinguishes 'strong' alignment (requiring genuine causal and intentional reasoning) from 'weak' alignment (statistically plausible outputs), showing that current LLMs like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Copilot fail strong alignment tests and represent human values differently in their embeddings.

papers.title: Are Large Language Models Consistent over Value-laden Questions?
papers.summary: Testing 8,000 value-laden questions across 300+ topics on models including Llama-3 and GPT-4o, the study finds LLMs are broadly consistent across paraphrases, formats, and translations, but fine-tuned models show topic-dependent inconsistencies similar to human variation on controversial subjects.

papers.title: CLAVE: An Adaptive Framework for Evaluating Values of LLM Generated Responses
papers.summary: CLAVE pairs a large LLM (for extracting value concepts) with a small fine-tuned model (for human-label calibration) to evaluate whether LLM responses align with human values; it also introduces ValEval, a dataset of 13k+ labeled examples across three value systems. Published at NeurIPS 2024.

papers.title: Do LLMs have Consistent Values?
papers.summary: Investigates whether LLMs exhibit consistent, human-like value hierarchies and finds results depend heavily on prompting strategy; introduces a 'Value Anchoring' technique that brings LLM value structures closer to human psychological data. Published at ICLR 2025.

papers.title: LocalValueBench: A Collaboratively Built and Extensible Benchmark for Evaluating Localized Value Alignment and Ethical Safety in Large Language Models
papers.summary: LocalValueBench is a collaboratively built benchmark that evaluates LLM alignment with Australian local values and ethical norms, offering a replicable methodology for regulators globally to create similar localized benchmarks.

papers.title: BLEnD: A Benchmark for LLMs on Everyday Knowledge in Diverse Cultures and Languages
papers.summary: BLEnD is a hand-crafted benchmark of 52.6k QA pairs covering everyday cultural knowledge from 16 countries in 13 languages, revealing that LLMs perform significantly worse on cultures with less online presence and on low-resource languages. Published at NeurIPS 2024.

papers.title: Culturally Aware and Adapted NLP: A Taxonomy and a Survey of the State of the Art
papers.summary: Proposes a fine-grained taxonomy of cultural elements in NLP and surveys existing resources and methods for culturally aware systems, mapping achievements and open research gaps across the field. Published in TACL.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Surveys, Taxonomies & Position Papers

papers.title: From Distributional to Overton Pluralism: Investigating Large Language Model Alignment
papers.summary: Analyzes how alignment fine-tuning changes LLM output distributions, finding that apparent diversity reductions are largely explained by quality control and information aggregation rather than suppression. Introduces the concept of a shift from distributional to Overton pluralism and shows aligned model behavior is recoverable from base models via in-context prompting.

papers.title: Modular Pluralism: Pluralistic Alignment via Multi-LLM Collaboration
papers.summary: Proposes Modular Pluralism, a framework that integrates smaller specialized community LMs with a base LLM to support three modes of pluralism (Overton, steerable, distributional), without requiring fine-tuning of the base model. Demonstrates effectiveness across six tasks and works with both proprietary and open-source LLMs.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Methods: Training, Steering & Adaptation

papers.title: Position: Towards Bidirectional Human-AI Alignment
papers.summary: Surveys over 400 papers to argue for a bidirectional conception of human-AI alignment that addresses not only aligning AI to human values but also how AI systems shape human cognition and behavior. Identifies gaps in long-term interaction design and mutual understanding, proposing a framework for reciprocal alignment research.

papers.title: ProgressGym: Alignment with a Millennium of Moral Progress
papers.summary: Introduces ProgressGym, a framework for learning moral progress mechanics from nine centuries of historical text (1221–2022 AD) and 18 historical LLMs, enabling AI alignment algorithms to anticipate and track evolving human values rather than locking in contemporary moral blindspots. Defines three core challenges: following, predicting, and co-evolving with moral progress.

papers.title: Raising the Bar: Investigating the Values of Large Language Models via Generative Evolving Testing
papers.summary: Proposes GETA (Generative Evolving Testing of vAlues), a framework that dynamically generates value-alignment test items tailored to each model's capability level, co-evolving with LLMs to avoid benchmark saturation and data leakage. Demonstrates more consistent evaluation results compared to static benchmarks.

papers.title: The Potential and Challenges of Evaluating Attitudes, Opinions, and Values in Large Language Models
papers.summary: A systematic overview of methods for evaluating attitudes, opinions, and values (AOVs) embedded in LLMs, highlighting that different evaluation approaches yield inconsistent results and offering practical recommendations for methodology and cross-disciplinary research.

papers.title: ValueBench: Towards Comprehensively Evaluating Value Orientations and Understanding of Large Language Models
papers.summary: ValueBench introduces the first comprehensive psychometric benchmark for evaluating LLM value orientations and understanding, drawing on 44 psychometric inventories across 453 value dimensions and testing six representative LLMs.

papers.title: CIVICS: Building a Dataset for Examining Culturally-Informed Values in Large Language Models
papers.summary: CIVICS is a hand-crafted multilingual dataset of prompts on value-sensitive topics (e.g., LGBTQI rights, immigration) designed to evaluate how LLMs respond to culturally-informed values across languages, revealing significant variation in model refusals by language and topic.

papers.title: CulturePark: Boosting Cross-cultural Understanding in Large Language Models
papers.summary: CulturePark is a multi-agent LLM communication framework that simulates cross-cultural dialogues to generate training data, producing culture-specific fine-tuned models that match or outperform GPT-4 on cultural alignment and content moderation tasks.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Methods: Training, Steering & Adaptation

papers.title: CultureBank: An Online Community-Driven Knowledge Base toward Culturally Aware Language Technologies
papers.summary: CultureBank is a large-scale cultural knowledge base of 23K descriptors derived from TikTok and Reddit user narratives, enabling evaluation of LLM cultural awareness and fine-tuning for improved performance on downstream cultural tasks.

papers.title: High-Dimension Human Value Representation in Large Language Models
papers.summary: UniVaR introduces a continuous, scalable high-dimensional representation of human value distributions in LLMs, trained via self-supervised value embedding learning and evaluated across 15 LLMs in 25 languages and cultures.

papers.title: NormAd: A Framework for Measuring the Cultural Adaptability of Large Language Models
papers.summary: NormAd is a framework and benchmark (2.6k situational descriptions from 75 countries) for evaluating LLMs' ability to judge social acceptability under varying levels of cultural norm specificity, revealing that models significantly lag behind humans especially for non-English-centric cultures.

papers.title: Social Choice Should Guide AI Alignment in Dealing with Diverse Human Feedback
papers.summary: Argues that social choice theory — the study of preference aggregation in collective decisions — should guide how RLHF and related methods handle diverse, conflicting human feedback when aligning foundation models.

papers.title: The PRISM Alignment Dataset: What Participatory, Representative and Individualised Human Feedback Reveals About the Subjective and Multicultural Alignment of Large Language Models
papers.summary: PRISM is a dataset of 8,011 conversations with 21 LLMs rated by 1,500 demographically diverse participants from 75 countries, enabling study of subjective, multicultural, and individualized alignment preferences.

papers.title: WorldValuesBench: A Large-Scale Benchmark for Multi-Cultural Value Awareness of Language Models
papers.summary: WorldValuesBench constructs over 20 million (demographic attributes, value question) → answer examples from the World Values Survey to benchmark LLMs' multi-cultural value awareness, finding even strong models struggle with the task.

papers.title: MoralBERT: A Fine-Tuned Language Model for Capturing Moral Values in Social Discussions
papers.summary: MoralBERT fine-tunes BERT-based models on multi-platform social media data annotated with Moral Foundations Theory labels, achieving 11-32% higher F1 than lexicon-based baselines and comparable performance to GPT-4 zero-shot for detecting moral sentiment in social discourse.

papers.title: Towards Measuring and Modeling "Culture" in LLMs: A Survey
papers.summary: A survey of 90+ papers on cultural representation in LLMs, organizing probing approaches along demographic and semantic dimensions, finding that only certain cultural aspects (mainly values) have been studied while many facets remain unexplored.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Surveys, Taxonomies & Position Papers

papers.title: What are human values, and how do we align AI to them?
papers.summary: Proposes a framework that decomposes AI-human values alignment into elicitation, reconciliation, and training stages, introducing Moral Graph Elicitation (MGE) — an LLM-mediated interview process — and validating it with 500 Americans, of whom 89% felt well represented.

papers.title: A Roadmap to Pluralistic Alignment
papers.summary: A position paper that formalizes three approaches to pluralistic alignment in LLMs — Overton (presenting a spectrum of reasonable views), steerable (adjustable per perspective), and distributional (calibrated to a population) — and outlines a research roadmap for each.

papers.title: CultureLLM: Incorporating Cultural Differences into Large Language Models
papers.summary: CultureLLM uses semantic data augmentation from the World Values Survey on just 50 seed samples per culture to fine-tune LLMs covering 9 cultures, outperforming GPT-3.5 by 8.1% and Gemini Pro by 9.5% on cultural understanding tasks.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Methods: Training, Steering & Adaptation

papers.title: Exploring Multilingual Concepts of Human Value in Large Language Models: Is Value Alignment Consistent, Transferable and Controllable across Languages?
papers.summary: Analyzes how LLMs represent human value concepts across 7 value types, 16 languages, and 3 LLM series, finding cross-lingual inconsistency, distorted linguistic relationships, and unidirectional transfer from high- to low-resource languages. The study reveals that multilingual value alignment is neither consistent nor freely transferable across languages.

papers.title: Investigating Cultural Alignment of Large Language Models
papers.summary: Measures cultural alignment of LLMs by simulating sociological surveys for Egypt and the US, finding that models align better when prompted in a culture's dominant language and pretrained on that culture's language mix. Introduces Anthropological Prompting to improve cultural alignment, highlighting worse misalignment for underrepresented groups.

papers.title: Investigating Human Values in Online Communities
papers.summary: Trains value relevance and polarity classifiers to automatically annotate over 6 million Reddit posts across 12,000 subreddits with Schwartz values, uncovering patterns such as strong opposition to conformity in certain communities and correlations between traditional values and conservative U.S. states.

papers.title: Morality is Non-Binary: Building a Pluralist Moral Sentence Embedding Space using Contrastive Learning
papers.summary: Builds a pluralist moral sentence embedding space using contrastive learning to capture multiple moral dimensions rather than binary right/wrong judgments, finding that moral pluralism requires supervised learning with human labels and cannot be recovered through self-supervision alone.

papers.title: Aligning Large Language Models with Human Opinions through Persona Selection and Value--Belief--Norm Reasoning
papers.summary: Proposes Chain-of-Opinion (COO), a four-step method grounded in Value-Belief-Norm theory for aligning LLMs with human opinions by filtering irrelevant persona attributes and applying structured reasoning, achieving up to 4% prompting gains and 23% gains when fine-tuning on COO-generated data.

papers.title: CDEval: A Benchmark for Measuring the Cultural Dimensions of Large Language Models
papers.summary: CDEval is a benchmark for measuring cultural dimensions of LLMs, covering six cultural aspects across seven domains, constructed via GPT-4 automated generation and human verification. It reveals that cultural considerations are underweighted in mainstream LLMs relative to universal alignment principles.

papers.title: Cultural Bias and Cultural Alignment of Large Language Models
papers.summary: Examines cultural biases in five OpenAI language models, finding they systematically favor values of English-speaking and Protestant European countries. Tests cultural prompting as a mitigation strategy, successfully improving alignment with local values in 71-81% of countries.

papers.title: Value FULCRA: Mapping Large Language Models to the Multidimensional Spectrum of Basic Human Values
papers.summary: Proposes FULCRA, a framework grounding LLM value alignment in Schwartz's Theory of Basic Human Values, mapping LLM outputs to a multidimensional value space. The accompanying 20k-pair dataset and BaseAlign paradigm outperform existing binary-label alignment approaches while anticipating previously unidentified risks.

papers.title: AI Alignment and Social Choice: Fundamental Limitations and Policy Implications
papers.summary: Applies social choice theory to AI alignment via RLHF, proving there is no universal voting protocol that can democratically align AI systems without violating individual users' private ethical preferences. Recommends transparency in voting rules and developing narrowly targeted AI agents.

papers.title: AI Alignment: A Comprehensive Survey
papers.summary: A comprehensive survey of AI alignment research organized around four core objectives (Robustness, Interpretability, Controllability, Ethicality) and two components: forward alignment (training) and backward alignment (assessment and governance). Provides a structured overview of techniques, benchmarks, and open challenges across the field.

papers.title: Do Differences in Values Influence Disagreements in Online Discussions?
papers.authors: van der Meer, Vossen, Jonker et al.
papers.summary: Investigates whether differences in personal values predict disagreement in online discussions, finding that value profile dissimilarity correlates with disagreement in specific contexts and that adding value information improves agreement-prediction models.

papers.title: EtiCor: Corpus for Analyzing LLMs for Etiquettes
papers.summary: Introduces EtiCor, a corpus of region-specific etiquette texts from five global regions, and proposes an Etiquette Sensitivity task; experiments show that state-of-the-art LLMs largely fail to understand etiquettes from non-Western cultures.

papers.title: Value Kaleidoscope: Engaging AI with Pluralistic Human Values, Rights, and Duties
papers.summary: Presents ValuePrism, a dataset of 218k values linked to 31k real-world situations, and Kaleido, a multi-task model that generates and assesses the relevance of pluralistic human values, rights, and duties in context.
subcategories: Overton Pluralism — Similar View: Covering the Full Spectrum

papers.title: ValueDCG: Measuring Comprehensive Human Value Understanding Ability of Language Models
papers.summary: Proposes ValueDCG, a metric evaluating LLMs on both factual and reasoned human value understanding, revealing that larger models paradoxically show declining value comprehension despite generating plausible explanations.

papers.title: Cultural Alignment in Large Language Models: An Explanatory Analysis Based on Hofstede's Cultural Dimensions
papers.summary: Designs a Cultural Alignment Test based on Hofstede's cultural dimensions and evaluates Llama 2, GPT-3.5, and GPT-4 across U.S., Chinese, and Arab cultural contexts, finding that all models struggle with cultural values but GPT-4 adapts best to Chinese contexts.

papers.title: From Instructions to Intrinsic Human Values -- A Survey of Alignment Goals for Big Models
papers.summary: A survey of alignment goals for large models that categorizes objectives into three levels (instructions, preferences, and intrinsic human values) and argues that centering alignment on intrinsic human values is the most promising direction for future work.

papers.title: From Values to Opinions: Predicting Human Behaviors and Stances Using Value-Injected Large Language Models
papers.summary: Introduces a Value Injection Method (VIM) that fine-tunes LLMs with targeted value distributions via argument generation and question answering, showing that value-injected LLMs substantially outperform baselines on predicting human opinions and stances.

papers.title: Rethinking Machine Ethics -- Can LLMs Perform Moral Reasoning through the Lens of Moral Theories?
papers.org: The Chinese University of Hong Kong
papers.summary: Proposes a top-down framework that steers LLMs to perform moral reasoning through established philosophical theories rather than crowd-sourced annotations, improving generalizability and explainability of moral judgments.

papers.title: Aligning Large Language Models with Human: A Survey
papers.summary: A survey of techniques for aligning LLMs with human expectations, covering instruction data collection, supervised fine-tuning, preference-based training, and evaluation methods for assessing aligned models.

papers.title: Large Language Models as Superpositions of Cultural Perspectives
papers.summary: Frames LLMs as superpositions of cultural perspectives and introduces the concept of perspective controllability, showing through psychological questionnaires that models exhibit context-dependent values and personality traits depending on the induced perspective.

papers.title: SemEval-2023 Task 4: ValueEval: Identification of Human Values Behind Arguments
papers.summary: ValueEval is a shared task at SemEval-2023 that challenged 39 teams to identify 20 human values (e.g., security, hedonism) underlying natural-language arguments in a 9K+ text dataset, with best systems reaching F1 of 0.87 on some value categories.

papers.title: What does a Text Classifier Learn about Morality? An Explainable Method for Cross-Domain Comparison of Moral Rhetoric
papers.summary: Introduces TOMEA, an explainable method that probes what moral knowledge a text classifier acquires, enabling cross-domain comparison of moral rhetoric across 35,000 tweets from seven domains.

papers.title: (GlobalOpinionQA) Towards Measuring the Representation of Subjective Global Opinions in Language Models
papers.summary: Proposes GlobalOpinionQA, a dataset from cross-national surveys, and a quantitative framework for measuring whether LLMs reflect global opinion diversity, finding that current models are biased toward US and select European/South American perspectives.

papers.title: (MoralChoice) Evaluating the Moral Beliefs Encoded in LLMs
papers.summary: Presents MoralChoice, a statistical survey of 680 high-ambiguity and 687 low-ambiguity moral scenarios administered to 28 LLMs, finding that models handle clear ethical choices well but show sensitivity to wording on ambiguous ones, with closed-source models clustering together.

papers.title: DLAMA: A Framework for Curating Culturally Diverse Facts for Probing the Knowledge of Pretrained LMs
papers.summary: DLAMA is a framework and benchmark of 78K culturally diverse factual triples spanning Arab, Asian, and South American vs. Western country pairs, revealing that multilingual LMs consistently perform better on Western facts.

papers.title: Heterogeneous Value Alignment Evaluation for Large Language Models
papers.summary: Proposes HVAE, a framework adapting Social Value Orientation from psychology to evaluate whether LLMs behave consistently with assigned social values. Finds that LLMs tend to gravitate toward neutral values rather than strong personal values when induced with heterogeneous value profiles.

papers.title: NormBank: A Knowledge Bank of Situational Social Norms
papers.summary: Introduces NormBank, a 155k-entry knowledge bank of situational social norms grounded in multivalent sociocultural frames (setting, agent roles, attributes, constraints). Demonstrates that neural models can extend its coverage and that it transfers effectively to downstream normative reasoning tasks.

papers.title: Assessing Cross-Cultural Alignment between ChatGPT and Human Societies
papers.summary: Empirically tests ChatGPT's cultural alignment across multiple societies and finds it strongly aligns with American cultural values when given American context but adapts poorly to other cultures. English prompts flatten cross-cultural variance and bias responses toward American norms.

papers.title: Personalisation within bounds: A risk taxonomy and policy framework for the alignment of large language models with personalised feedback
papers.summary: Develops a risk taxonomy and three-tiered policy framework for personalizing LLMs with user-specific feedback while bounding unsafe behaviors within organizational and societal limits. Argues that current aggregate alignment approaches fail to represent the full diversity of user values and preferences.

papers.title: (Valueeval) The Touché23-ValueEval Dataset for Identifying Human Values behind Arguments
papers.summary: Presents the Touché23-ValueEval dataset of 9,324 arguments from six diverse sources, each annotated by crowdworkers for 54 human values, expanding the prior dataset and supporting the SemEval 2023 ValueEval shared task. The larger dataset improves model training despite increased classification difficulty.

papers.title: The Moral Foundations Reddit Corpus
papers.summary: Introduces a corpus of 16,123 Reddit comments annotated for moral sentiment across 8 categories from Moral Foundations Theory, enabling computational detection of moral rhetoric with baseline evaluations using LLMs and fine-tuned encoders.

papers.title: ProsocialDialog: A Prosocial Backbone for Conversational Agents
papers.org: Seoul National University / Allen Institute for AI
papers.summary: Presents ProsocialDialog, a large-scale dataset of 58K dialogues with 331K utterances designed to teach conversational agents to respond safely to problematic user inputs, along with two models—Canary for safety detection and Prost for generating socially-informed responses.

papers.title: The Moral Integrity Corpus: A Benchmark for Ethical Dialogue Systems
papers.summary: Introduces the Moral Integrity Corpus (MIC), a benchmark of 38K chatbot prompt-reply pairs annotated with 99K Rules of Thumb capturing moral convictions, used to evaluate language models on classifying and generating ethical judgments in dialogue.

papers.title: Challenges and Strategies in Cross-Cultural NLP
papers.summary: Surveys the challenges of building NLP systems that account for cultural variation beyond language, proposing a framework of cross-cultural and multicultural NLP strategies to address how cultural context shapes language use and content.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Surveys, Taxonomies & Position Papers

papers.title: Probing Pre-Trained Language Models for Cross-Cultural Differences in Values
papers.summary: Uses probing methods to investigate whether pre-trained language models encode cross-cultural value differences, finding that models do capture some cultural variation but their embedded values align only weakly with established cross-cultural survey frameworks.

papers.title: ValueNet: A New Dataset for Human Value Driven Dialogue System
papers.summary: ValueNet introduces a large-scale dataset of 21,374 text scenarios annotated with human attitudes across ten value dimensions, and uses it to train a Transformer-based value model that improves personalized dialogue generation and empathetic response tasks.

papers.title: Moral Stories: Situated Reasoning about Norms, Intents, Actions, and their Consequences
papers.authors: Emelin, Le Bras, Hwang et al.
papers.summary: Moral Stories provides a crowd-sourced dataset of structured branching narratives to study whether language models can generate norm-constrained actions, anticipate consequences of moral or immoral behavior, and produce explanatory social norms.

papers.title: Social Chemistry 101: Learning to Reason about Social and Moral Norms
papers.summary: Social Chemistry 101 introduces Social-Chem-101, a corpus of 292k rules-of-thumb with 4.5 million annotations across 12 moral and social dimensions, and demonstrates that a Neural Norm Transformer can learn to generate contextually appropriate social rules for novel situations.

papers.title: (ETHICS) Aligning AI With Shared Human Values
papers.summary: The ETHICS benchmark introduces a dataset spanning justice, well-being, duties, virtues, and commonsense morality to evaluate whether language models can predict human ethical judgments, finding that current models only partially succeed.

papers.title: Scruples: A Corpus of Community Ethical Judgments on 32,000 Real-Life Anecdotes
papers.summary: Scruples presents a corpus of 625,000 community ethical judgments over 32,000 real-life anecdotes and shows that neural models learn simpler ethical patterns but struggle with complex moral dilemmas that naturally produce human disagreement.

papers.title: Moral Foundations Twitter Corpus: A Collection of 35k Tweets Annotated for Moral Sentiment
papers.summary: A dataset of 35,108 tweets annotated for moral sentiment across 10 categories from Moral Foundations Theory, with baseline model results and annotator psychological profiles to support research on moral sentiment detection.

papers.title: A 30-year struggle; the sustained efforts to give force of law to the Universal Declaration of Human Rights
papers.summary: Karel Vasak's 1977 essay chronicles the thirty-year campaign to transform the Universal Declaration of Human Rights from a non-binding declaration into enforceable international law, introducing his influential three-generations framework for human rights.
subcategories: Political, Civic Values and Human Rights

papers.title: A theory of justice.
papers.summary: John Rawls' foundational 1971 work develops a theory of justice as fairness, arguing that principles of justice are those rational agents would choose from behind a 'veil of ignorance', offering a Kantian alternative to utilitarianism.
subcategories: Political, Civic Values and Human Rights

papers.title: Aggregating Sets of Judgments: An Impossibility Result
papers.summary: List and Pettit prove a general impossibility theorem showing that no aggregation procedure can consistently combine individually coherent sets of judgments on logically interconnected questions into a coherent group judgment, paralleling Arrow's impossibility theorem.

papers.title: Alignment-Goal-Survey
papers.summary: A curated repository accompanying a survey that traces the evolution of LLM alignment goals from instruction-following through human preferences to intrinsic human values, arguing that grounding alignment in core human values is the most promising direction.

papers.title: An Overview of the Schwartz Theory of Basic Values
papers.summary: An overview of Schwartz's theory identifying ten universal basic human values organized in a circular motivational structure, validated across 82 countries using two measurement instruments (Schwartz Value Survey and Portrait Values Questionnaire).

papers.title: Awesome-LLM-in-Social-Science
papers.summary: A curated reading list of papers at the intersection of LLMs and social science, covering value/personality evaluation, moral reasoning, opinion measurement, and agent-based social simulation.

papers.title: Awesome-LLM-Psychometrics
papers.summary: A curated resource collection accompanying a systematic review on LLM psychometrics, organizing papers on how psychological assessment tools measure personality, values, moral reasoning, and cognitive biases in language models.

papers.title: awesome-llm-social-simulation
papers.summary: A curated reading list of research on using LLMs to simulate social systems and human behavior at scale, spanning simulation platforms, domain applications, and critical evaluations of LLM-based multi-agent social models.

papers.title: Awesome-Personalized-Alignment
papers.summary: A continually updated curated list of papers on personalized alignment in AI, focusing on building systems that adapt to individual user preferences through personalized preference learning and user-centric LLM alignment methods.

papers.title: Awesome-Pluralistic-Alignment
papers.summary: A curated GitHub repository of research resources on pluralistic alignment for LLMs, organizing methodologies, datasets, and benchmarks across consensus-based, steerable, distributional, and multi-mode alignment approaches.

papers.title: Basic human values: Theory, measurement, and applications
papers.summary: Schwartz presents a cross-culturally validated theory of 10 motivationally distinct basic human values, with measurement instruments tested across 70+ countries, revealing a universal structure of value conflict and congruence.

papers.title: Citizenship and Social Class
papers.summary: Marshall's seminal sociological essay analyzes citizenship as comprising civil, political, and social rights, tracing their historical development in postwar Britain; Bottomore's accompanying essay revisits these themes in contemporary contexts.
subcategories: Political, Civic Values and Human Rights

papers.title: Collective Choice and Social Welfare
papers.summary: Sen's foundational work examines the ethical and mathematical foundations of collective decision-making and social welfare, arguing that Arrow's impossibility theorems do not preclude reasoned democratic social choice.

papers.title: Conflicts of Values (in *Moral Luck*)
papers.summary: Williams examines Berlin's value pluralism—the view that multiple irreducibly conflicting values exist—and argues that defending plurality itself becomes a necessary political task requiring active engagement rather than passive observation.

papers.title: Creating Capabilities: The Human Development Approach and Its Implementation
papers.summary: Nussbaum presents her Capabilities Approach as an alternative framework for human development, arguing that standard economic theories fail to capture what people are actually able to do and be, and proposes a list of central human capabilities as a basis for political principles and social justice.
subcategories: Political, Civic Values and Human Rights

papers.title: Cultural Value Orientations
papers.summary: Schwartz proposes a theory of seven cultural value orientations (e.g., Embeddedness, Hierarchy, Mastery, Harmony) derived from cross-national survey data, and examines how these orientations correlate with national policies, economic systems, and societal outcomes.

papers.title: cultural-llm-papers
papers.summary: A curated reading list of research papers on culture and LLMs, organized by definition-based and methods-based taxonomies, serving as a companion resource to the survey 'Towards Measuring and Modeling Culture in LLMs'.

papers.title: culture-awareness-llms
papers.summary: A curated repository accompanying the survey 'Survey of Cultural Awareness in Language Models: Text and Beyond' (arXiv:2411.00860), cataloging methods, datasets, and benchmarks for making LLMs more culturally sensitive across text and multimodal settings.

papers.title: Culture's consequences: International differences in work-related values
papers.summary: Hofstede's landmark cross-national study of IBM employees in 50+ countries identifies four dimensions of national culture (Power Distance, Individualism, Uncertainty Avoidance, Masculinity) and demonstrates how these dimensions predict systematic differences in work-related values and behavior across societies.

papers.title: Cultures and organizations: software of the mind
papers.summary: A foundational cross-cultural psychology book proposing dimensional frameworks (such as power distance and individualism) derived from four decades of survey research across 70+ countries to explain how national culture shapes values, thinking, and behavior. It distinguishes national from organizational culture and provides tools for cross-cultural management and communication.

papers.title: ESS — European Social Survey
papers.summary: The European Social Survey (ESS) is a pan-European, biennial cross-national survey infrastructure collecting freely accessible data on attitudes, behaviors, and social values across 25+ European nations since 2001, used widely in social science research.

papers.title: EVS — European Values Survey
papers.summary: The European Values Study (EVS) is a large-scale, repeated cross-sectional survey program tracking basic human values — including family, religion, work, and politics — across European countries from 1981 to 2020, with data archived at GESIS.

papers.title: Functional theory of human values
papers.summary: Gouveia et al. propose and empirically test a functional theory of human values organized around a 3×2 framework of six subfunctions (personal, central, and social goals crossed with survival and thriving needs), validating content and structure hypotheses in a large Brazilian sample (N=13,414).

papers.title: GSS — General Social Survey
papers.org: NORC at the University of Chicago
papers.summary: The General Social Survey (GSS) is a continuously administered (since 1972) national survey by NORC at the University of Chicago that tracks American public attitudes, behaviors, and social values, serving as a key longitudinal dataset for social science research.

papers.title: Handbook of Computational Social Choice
papers.summary: A comprehensive reference covering the intersection of computer science and economics in collective decision-making, including voting theory, fair allocation, coalition formation, and judgment aggregation, contributed by 36 leading researchers.

papers.title: Liberal Pluralism: The Implications of Value Pluralism for Political Theory and Practice
papers.summary: Galston argues that liberalism is compatible with Isaiah Berlin's value pluralism, developing a 'liberal pluralism' framework that allows diverse conceptions of human flourishing to coexist within liberal political institutions.

papers.title: Liberals and conservatives rely on different sets of moral foundations
papers.summary: Across four studies, liberals were found to prioritize harm and fairness moral foundations while conservatives drew more equally on all five foundations (harm, fairness, loyalty, authority, sanctity), explaining the intractability of political moral disagreements.

papers.title: Manipulation of Voting Schemes: A General Result
papers.summary: Gibbard proves that any non-trivial voting scheme without chance elements can be strategically manipulated — a voter can secure a preferred outcome by misrepresenting their preferences — establishing a foundational impossibility result in social choice theory.

papers.title: Mapping and interpreting cultural differences around the world
papers.summary: Inglehart and Welzel map global cultural variation along two dimensions — traditional vs. secular-rational values and survival vs. self-expression values — using World Values Survey data to show systematic cross-national patterns in human belief systems.

papers.title: Measuring the Refined Theory of Individual Values in 49 Cultural Groups
papers.summary: Validates the Revised Portrait Value Questionnaire (PVQ-RR) for measuring Schwartz's 19 refined individual values across 49 cultural groups and 32 language versions, demonstrating reliable measurement and cross-cultural invariance in a sample of 53,472 participants.

papers.title: Mental representations of social values.
papers.summary: Reviews the psychological functioning of social values by conceptualizing them as mental representations operating at system, abstract, and instantiation levels, clarifying how values influence attitudes and behavior.

papers.title: Modernization and Postmodernization: Cultural, Economic, and Political Change in 43 Societies
papers.summary: Argues using World Values Survey data from 43 societies that economic development, cultural change, and political change co-occur in predictable patterns, tracing a shift from modern to postmodern values as societies industrialize and then advance further.

papers.title: Modernization, Cultural Change, and Democracy
papers.summary: Presents a revised modernization theory showing that socioeconomic development systematically shifts populations from survival to self-expression values, and that this cultural change is a key driver of democratization, tested across 85% of the world's population via the World Values Survey.

papers.title: Moral Foundations Theory: The Pragmatic Validity of Moral Pluralism
papers.summary: Presents and defends Moral Foundations Theory (MFT), arguing that human moral psychology is built on multiple innate foundations shaped by culture, and that this moral pluralism produces pragmatically valid and empirically productive research across political and social psychology.

papers.title: On the Rationale of Group Decision-making
papers.summary: Duncan Black's 1948 foundational paper introduces the median voter theorem and single-peaked preferences to show how majority voting can yield consistent collective decisions when individual preferences are suitably structured.

papers.title: Refining the theory of basic individual values
papers.summary: Schwartz et al. refine the classic theory of basic human values by expanding the original 10-value model to 19 more finely differentiated values, validating the updated circular motivational continuum across 15 samples from 10 countries.

papers.title: Social Choice and Individual Values
papers.summary: Kenneth Arrow's landmark monograph proves the impossibility theorem: no method of aggregating individual preference orderings into a social choice can simultaneously satisfy a minimal set of fairness conditions (Pareto, independence of irrelevant alternatives, non-dictatorship) when there are three or more alternatives.

papers.title: Social Choice Theory (in Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy)
papers.summary: Christian List's Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy entry surveys social choice theory — from Condorcet and Arrow's impossibility results to escape routes via restricted domains, interpersonal welfare comparisons, and judgment aggregation — as a framework for collective decision-making.

papers.title: SocialAgent
papers.summary: A curated resource repository accompanying a survey on LLM-based social simulation, covering individual behavior modeling, scenario-driven agent interactions, and society-level dynamics across economics, sociology, and online platforms.

papers.title: Strategy-proofness and Arrow's Conditions
papers.summary: Satterthwaite proves that any strategy-proof voting procedure over three or more alternatives must be dictatorial, establishing a formal correspondence between strategy-proofness in voting and Arrow's conditions for social welfare functions.

papers.title: The Impossibility of a Paretian Liberal
papers.summary: Sen demonstrates a fundamental impossibility: no social choice rule can simultaneously satisfy even a minimal condition of individual liberty (liberalism) and the Pareto principle, showing a deep tension between freedom and welfare-based social choice.

papers.title: The Morality of Freedom
papers.summary: Raz's influential book argues that personal autonomy is the core of liberal morality and that the state has a duty to promote conditions for autonomous living, examining authority, rights, equality, and value pluralism in a comprehensive theory of political morality.

papers.title: The Morality of Pluralism
papers.summary: Kekes defends ethical pluralism — the view that there are many genuinely valid but irreducibly diverse systems of values — against both moral dogmatism and relativism, arguing this provides a better foundation for understanding good lives and political morality.

papers.title: The Morals of Modernity
papers.summary: Larmore examines how modern moral philosophy should be shaped by the conditions of modernity, critiquing attempts to revive ancient virtue ethics and developing foundations for political liberalism that rest on moral pluralism and the limits of reason.

papers.title: The nature of human values.
papers.summary: Milton Rokeach's foundational 1973 book proposes that a small set of terminal and instrumental values serve as internal reference points guiding attitudes and behavior, and introduces the Rokeach Value Survey to measure their relative rankings.

papers.title: The Right and the Good
papers.summary: W.D. Ross's 1930 classic in analytic ethics develops a pluralist deontological theory of prima facie duties, arguing that rightness cannot be reduced to promoting goodness and that multiple irreducible moral obligations coexist.

papers.title: The Righteous Mind
papers.summary: Jonathan Haidt's book argues that moral judgments are primarily driven by intuition rather than reasoning, and that differing moral foundations — across care, fairness, loyalty, authority, and purity — explain political and religious divisions.

papers.title: The Theory of Communicative Action
papers.summary: Jürgen Habermas's 1984 two-volume work develops a theory of communicative rationality, arguing that social coordination and modernity are best understood through the intersubjective structure of communicative action rather than purely instrumental reason.
subcategories: Political, Civic Values and Human Rights

papers.title: The theory of dyadic morality: Reinventing moral judgment by redefining harm.
papers.summary: Schein and Gray propose the Theory of Dyadic Morality, which redefines harm as an intuitively perceived continuum involving an agent causing damage to a patient, and argues this dyadic structure underlies moral condemnation and explains phenomena like moral acquisition and polarization.

papers.title: Towards Realistic Evaluation of Cultural Value Alignment: Diversity Enhancement for Survey Simulation
papers.summary: Proposes a diversity-enhancement framework with a memory simulation mechanism to more realistically evaluate how well LLMs align with cultural values by simulating sociological survey responses; reveals divergence and preference biases across models in US and Chinese contexts.
subcategories: Multicultural Alignment — Methods: Training, Steering & Adaptation

papers.title: Two Concepts of Liberty
papers.summary: Isaiah Berlin's landmark 1958 Oxford lecture distinguishes negative liberty (freedom from interference) from positive liberty (self-mastery), arguing that conflating the two enables authoritarian justifications for coercion and that irreducible value pluralism is central to political philosophy.

papers.title: Universals in the content and structure of values: Theoretical advances and empirical tests in 20 countries.
papers.summary: Schwartz's foundational 1992 theory identifies ten motivationally distinct universal value types and maps their structural relations, validated with empirical data across 20 countries.

papers.title: Value Pluralism (in Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy)
papers.summary: Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy entry by Elinor Mason surveying value pluralism — the view that there are multiple irreducible moral values — and the challenges this poses for rational choice between incommensurable values.

papers.title: WVS — World Values Survey
papers.summary: The World Values Survey is a global cross-national research project that collects longitudinal data on human values, beliefs, and attitudes across societies, serving as a widely-used empirical resource for studying cultural variation.
